message:
fix(dns): dual-clock timeout in ResolverTimeout for deterministic testing

ResolverTimeout now separates the authoritative timeout deadline
(computed from the injected time_getter) from the wake-capable sleep
(computed from the runtime/wall-clock time via timeout_now()). This
fixes an issue where virtual clock-driven resolvers would not get
woken because the Sleep was registered in a time domain the runtime
could not advance.

New timeout_now() helper reads the current Cx timer driver when
available, falling back to wall_now(). The deadline field is stored
separately for authoritative timeout decisions, while the sleep is
used solely for wake registration. When the wake sleep fires before
the injected clock reaches the deadline, rearm_wake_sleep() recalculates
the remaining duration and re-registers in the runtime time domain.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
